items.blurb: Celtics' Jayson Tatum addresses injury concerns ahead of Game 7 — Given that Tatum is coming off a major injury, it didn't take long before fans sounded the alarms. That's why he wanted to clear the air and ease everyone's minds.

items.blurb: ‘Blessed to have him’: Ja’Kobe Walter pinch hits for Ingram-less Raptors — Ja’Kobe Walter answered the call, finishing with 24 points, five rebounds, three steals and a block while shooting 7-of-13 from the field and 4-of-9 from three in 43 minutes.

items.blurb: Barrett has ‘Kawhi moment’ for hometown Raptors in Game 6 — Danielle Michaud and Michael Grange break down the Toronto Raptors’ 112-110 overtime win over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 6, discussing RJ Barrett’s “Kawhi moment” with a last-second game winner, and more.

items.headline: Duke To Play 3 Neutral-Site Games On Amazon Next Season
items.blurb: Duke To Play 3 Neutral-Site Games On Amazon Next Season — Duke have entered an enterprise partnership with Amazon that includes televising three games next season. The package includes neutral-site games against UConn on November 25, Michigan on December 21, and Gonzaga on February 20. items.headline: Raptors’ Barrett has confidence to ‘live and die’ by last-second shot
items.blurb: Raptors’ Barrett has confidence to ‘live and die’ by last-second shot — Toronto Raptors forward RJ Barrett discusses his last-second game winner in overtime against the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 6, saying he’s willing to “live and die” by those type of shots since he’s built up that confidence in his game.

items.headline: RJ Barrett's Clutch Three-Pointer Forces Game 7 As Raptors Beat Cavs In Overtime
items.blurb: RJ Barrett's Clutch Three-Pointer Forces Game 7 As Raptors Beat Cavs In Overtime — RJ Barrett hit a three-pointer with 1.2 seconds remaining in overtime to lift the Toronto Raptors past the Cleveland Cavaliers 112-110 in Game 6 Friday, forcing a decisive Game 7 in Cleveland. items.headline: Pistons Overcome Historic 24-Point Deficit To Force Game 7 Against Magic
items.blurb: Pistons Overcome Historic 24-Point Deficit To Force Game 7 Against Magic — Cade Cunningham scored 32 points and the Detroit Pistons mounted one of the most remarkable comebacks in NBA playoff history Friday night, erasing a 24-point deficit to defeat the Orlando Magic 93-79 and force a Game 7 in their Eastern Conference first-round series. items.blurb: Mark Cuban Admits He Tried To Buy Back Mavericks — Mark Cuban confirmed he was involved in an effort to reacquire the Dallas Mavericks from the Adelson family, but acknowledged the attempt went nowhere after the new ownership group made clear the franchise is not available, Cuban told Front Office Sports in an interview set to air on an upcoming episode of Portfolio Players. items.blurb: NBA Fines Mitchell Robinson $50,000, Dyson Daniels $25,000 Following Game 6 Ejections — The NBA fined New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson $50,000 and Atlanta Hawks guard Dyson Daniels $25,000 for their involvement in an on-court altercation during Game 6 of their first-round playoff series. items.headline: Gotta See It: Barrett gets friendly bounce to drain Game 6 OT winner
items.blurb: Gotta See It: Barrett gets friendly bounce to drain Game 6 OT winner — Watch as the Raptors force a turnover late in overtime, and set up RJ Barrett for the three, which takes a big bounce off the rim before going in to win and force Game 7 against the Cavaliers.

items.blurb: Barrett hits wild winner as Raptors beat Cavaliers in OT to force Game 7 — It was only one bounce and it was to extend the series, not win it. But RJ Barrett had his Kawhi Leonard moment.

items.blurb: No Anthony Edwards, no problem: Jaden McDaniels is the Timberwolves' man — The last time NBA fans saw Anthony Edwards on the hardcourt, he couldn’t walk on his own accord as trainers helped him get back to the locker room of Target Center.

items.headline: What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons
items.blurb: What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons — One minute into the second half of Game 6, the Orlando Magic had a 24-point lead and were poised to eliminate the top-seeded Detroit Pistons. They proceeded to score the fewest points ever in an NBA playoff half in a 93-79 loss.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic knows who to blame for Nuggets' epic playoff collapse
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic knows who to blame for Nuggets' epic playoff collapse — The Denver Nuggets have some tough decisions to make in the offseason. Failing to take down the No. 6 seed Minnesota Timberwolves without Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu might be the biggest blemish in Nikola Jokic's postseason career so far.

items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Out To Start Series Against Spurs — Anthony Edwards will be out to start their second round series with a bone bruise and a left knee hyperextension. Edwards suffered the injury in Game 4 last Saturday and the injury typically requires a minimum two-week recovery. items.headline: What's next for Nikola Jokic, Nuggets after stunning collapse vs. Timberwolves?
items.blurb: What's next for Nikola Jokic, Nuggets after stunning collapse vs. Timberwolves? — Nikola Jokic and the Denver Nuggets suffered an embarrassing playoff series loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves in six games. Minnesota was without star guard Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo for multiple games due to injuries, yet the Nuggets failed to capitalize on the Timberwolves' setbacks.

items.blurb: Why Barrett feels elimination games play right into Raptors’ personality — Raptors’ RJ Barrett talks about the team’s mindset heading into their first elimination game, says they’ve always been a team that fights and scraps, and thinks it fits right into their personality.

items.blurb: Dunc'd On: DEN | MIN Game 6, PHI | BOS Game 6, ATL | NYK Game 6 — The Minnesota Timberwolves took down their bitter rivals in inspiring, glorious fashion. items.headline: WNBA Franchise Values Rise 345 Percent Since 2024, Average Team Now Worth $427M
items.blurb: WNBA Franchise Values Rise 345 Percent Since 2024, Average Team Now Worth $427M — The average WNBA franchise is now valued at $427 million, a 59% increase from 2025 and a 345% surge from 2024, according to Sportico. The collective value of the league's 13 teams that played in 2025 stand at $5.55 billion, including real estate and related assets. The growth coincides with the league's 30th season,

items.headline: LeBron James: Leads Los Angeles to win
items.blurb: LeBron James: Leads Los Angeles to win — James finished with 28 points (10-25 FG, 2-5 3Pt, 6-8 FT), seven rebounds and eight assists across 37 minutes in Friday's 98-78 win over the Rockets in Game 6 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Alperen Sengun: Double-doubles in Game 6 loss
items.blurb: Alperen Sengun: Double-doubles in Game 6 loss — Sengun supplied 17 points (5-12 FG, 7-10 FT), 11 rebounds, two blocks and one assist across 38 minutes in Friday's 98-78 loss to the Lakers in Game 6 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum Not Concerned About Left Calf Tightness, Expects To Play Game 7
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum Not Concerned About Left Calf Tightness, Expects To Play Game 7 — Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum was spotted stretching out his left calf several times during his team's Game 6 loss at the Philadelphia 76ers. Tatum exited the contest for good in the third quarter and was later seen on the exercise bike. Despite the soreness in his left calf, Tatum expects to play in Game 7 on Sat

items.headline: Jaden McDaniels backs up his trash talk with magnificent Game 6 performance
items.blurb: Jaden McDaniels backs up his trash talk with magnificent Game 6 performance — McDaniels talked trash about the Denver Nuggets all series long. Then in Game 6, with three of his team's best players out, he dominated on both ends of the court and made good on all his boasting.

items.headline: RJ Barrett: Seals win in dramatic fashion
items.blurb: RJ Barrett: Seals win in dramatic fashion — Barrett produced 24 points (8-21 FG, 4-12 3Pt, 4-5 FT), nine rebounds, three assists and one steal over 43 minutes during Friday's 112-110 overtime victory over the Cavaliers in Game 6 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: James Harden: Flirts with triple-double in loss
items.blurb: James Harden: Flirts with triple-double in loss — Harden ended with 16 points (5-14 FG, 1-4 3Pt, 5-5 FT), nine rebounds, nine assists, one block and two steals across 44 minutes during Friday's 112-110 overtime loss to the Raptors in Game 6 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
